title: American Communities 1776-2026
description: Swarthmore College Libraries marks "America250" with a special exhibition in McCabe Library. Drawing on art and artifacts from Special Collections, the exhibition explores how family, land, and nation have shaped the idea of “America,” and invites visitors to consider how they see this country and its history today.

title: Summer Writing Group
description:
Join the Summer Writing Group for dedicated time and community around your summer projects. Whether you’re writing, reading, revising, or making steady progress on a goal, all faculty and staff are welcome.

We have a room reserved Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:00 a.m.–2:00 p.m. Participants are welcome to come and go as needed.

title: Hatha Yoga with Stacy Bomento
description: This Hatha yoga class will focus on breathing and mindful movement, helping to increase strength and flexibility. Offering modifications for various postures, this class is accessible to all levels of practice. Each practice will include poses, breathing exercises, and a restorative and calming finish, followed by guided meditation. **There will be no class on July 23**
location: Matchbox Wellness Lounge Upper Level, Matchbox Tarble Commons Upper Level

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: Balance and Boundaries
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Learn ways to set and maintain realistic boundaries to better manage your time and support your personal well-being. In this workshop, we will explore strategies to find balance and thrive in academic and professional settings.

Presented by Dean Liz Derickson, Associate Dean of Academic Success; Melissa Mandos, Director of Student Academic Mentors and Fellowships & Prizes Advisor; and Ashley Netanel, Associate Director of Health Promotion & Wellness Initiatives.

title: Group Circuit Training with Chris
description: Do you struggle to figure out which workout to do at the gym, or do you need some extra motivation? If so, this class is for you. Receive personal attention to help you improve your overall fitness through a total body workout. The class will offer modifications or challenges to suit each participant. Missed a workout? Review past workouts online.
location: Matchbox Lower Level Lewis Reynolds Performance Center

title: Pilates with Cathy Raymond
description:
Pilates is a progressive system of exercises designed to stretch, strengthen, and balance both mind and body. Benefits include increased core strength, better posture and balance skills, more physical and mental energy, and improved sports performance.

A recording of each class is available on YouTube.

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: Navigating Unexpected Challenges
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

We all know that life doesn’t always follow a script! This workshop will help you develop skills to navigate around obstacles or unexpected challenges. We’ll explore distress tolerance, emotion regulation, and how to ask for help so you can move from overwhelm into action.

Presented by Simone Collins, Director of Counseling & Psychological Services, and Josh Ellow, Associate Director, Wellness Assessment & Compliance & Alcohol & Other Drug Counselor.

title: Hatha Yoga with Stacy Bomento
description: This Hatha yoga class will focus on breathing and mindful movement, helping to increase strength and flexibility. Offering modifications for various postures, this class is accessible to all levels of practice. Each practice will include poses, breathing exercises, and a restorative and calming finish, followed by guided meditation. **There will be no class on July 21**
location: Matchbox Wellness Lounge Upper Level, Matchbox Tarble Commons Upper Level

title: Peter van de Kamp Observatory Open House
description:
The Physics and Astronomy department hosts a telescope open house on the second Tuesday of each month at the Peter van de Kamp Observatory on the roof of the Science Center. Directions are available at www.swarthmore.edu/peter-van-de-kamp-observatory/directions.

The open house hours are 8:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. during standard time and 9:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. during daylight savings time. The SC-1 exterior door under the breezeway will be open 10 minutes before the start time. The rooftop access door will open at the event start time.

If the weather is bad, the open house will be cancelled by 5:00 p.m., and an announcement will be posted on the Physics and Astronomy department website and the College calendar. Check https://www.swarthmore.edu/physics-astronomy on the day of the open house if the weather is questionable

Open to the public.
location: Science Center Peter Van de Kamp Observatory, Science Center Observatory Computer Room

title: From “Inaccessible” to Inclusive: A Practical PDF Remediation Demo
description:
From “Inaccessible” to Inclusive: A Practical PDF Remediation Demo with the WordPress Accessibility Meetup


Register for A Practical PDF Remediation Demo


Date & Time


Jul 21, 2026

11:00 AM


Description


PDFs are one of the most common and most misunderstood document formats when it comes to accessibility. In this practical, demo-led session, we’ll take a PDF and work through how to remediate it step by step. The session will focus on what good looks like, common pitfalls to avoid, and when a PDF is (and isn’t) the right format to use. You’ll leave with a clearer understanding of PDF accessibility and the confidence to start improving your own documents.

This meetup will be live captioned.


Speakers


Samantha Merrett


Senior Accessibility Specialist,

title: Summer 2026 Workshop Series: Telling your story
description:
Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sci 101 from 12-1:30pm. Lunch will be provided.

Now that you’re coming to the end of the summer program, this workshop will help you reflect upon the skills you’ve gained and how far you’ve come. We’ll explore how your experiences shape your self-understanding and how to integrate new skills into your personal narrative(s). Finally, we’ll consider how this reflection enables you to share your story impactfully as you take your next steps.

Presented by Susannah Fishman, Director, Center for Innovation & Leadership, and Rebecca Weintraub-Barth, Director of Student Activities & Leadership.

title: CCC 2026 Summer Concert
description:
Summer Concerts - 80s Party


Dust off your leg warmers and tease up your hair because the Chester Children's Chorus is heading back to the 1980s! Our summer concerts feature some of the decade's biggest hits from artists including Whitney Houston, Journey, Cyndi Lauper, Kool & the Gang, Bon Jovi, and Michael Jackson. 

 

It's a celebration of the music, energy, and fun that defined a generation, performed by 140 Chester children who have no idea what a mixtape is.
location: Lang Music Building Room 214-Presser Room, Lang Music Concert Hall, Lang Music Building Upper Lobby, Lang Music Building Lower Lobby

title: SwatTalk: In a League of Her Own: A Conversation with Women’s Baseball Pioneer Laura Hirai ’22
description:
SwatTalk: In a League of Her Own: A Conversation with Women’s Baseball Pioneer Laura Hirai ’22

Save the Date: July 28th 2026 at 12 p.m. ET

As a young girl, Laura Hirai ’22 fell in love with baseball. Growing up in Japan, she recalls her experience with the sport was not particularly gendered. It was not until she attempted to attend a baseball camp in the U.S., and was told it was only for boys, that she realized she may face some obstacles to achieving her diamond dreams. Like many Swarthmore students, Laura approached these obstacles as a challenge rather than a deterrent, and at 26 years old has become a global face of women's baseball. Her resumé includes being the first, and thus far only, woman to represent Great Britain in U18 baseball; becoming the first "ball girl" for the New York Yankees during the first Major League Baseball game played in England in 2019; and being drafted in the fifth round in the inaugural draft of the U.S.-based Women's Professional Baseball League, which is set to begin play in August. Laura also played varsity softball at Swarthmore. Please join Laura for a conversation with Ana Apostoleris Rivera '13 about her pioneering experiences and her insights on the future of women's baseball.

Laura Hirai '22 graduated with a Bachelor of Arts in Arabic and Spanish. A dual-sport athlete at the international level, she is also the first person to represent Great Britain in both baseball and softball simultaneously at the national level, pitching at the European Baseball Championships in 2015 and 2017. One of only three European athletes selected in the inaugural Women's Professional Baseball League draft, Laura most recently helped Great Britain claim gold in women's baseball and bronze in softball at the European Championships, with both teams earning World Cup berths.

More Information & Registration Coming Soon

title: Swarthmore Summer Scholars Program (S3P) Poster Session
description:
The Swarthmore Summer Scholar Program (S3P) invites the campus community to visit our Annual Poster Session on Friday, August 1st from 10am-11:30am in Singer Atrium.

This July, the S3P Scholars have been learning about how cells divide. For their culminating projects, they are designing, conducting, and analyzing their own experiments to examine the effects of chemicals on cell division. Please drop in anytime between 10am and 11:30am to meet this amazing cohort of incoming first-year students and see what they’ve discovered!

We are excited to see you there!

The Annual Swarthmore Summer Scholars Program Poster Session

Celebrating the S3P Class of 2029

Friday, August 1, 10am-11:30am, Singer Atrium

title: CCC Presents The Sky Brooks Quartet
description:
Enjoy an afternoon with The Skyy Brooks Quartet featuring jazz favorites and special arrangements, including "My Funny Valentine," "I Didn't Know What Time It Was," "How High The Moon," "If I Were A Bell," "Blackbird," and "A House Is Not A Home." Come hear Skyy and Chester Children's Chorus Executive and Artistic Director John Alston perform their very favorite songs, and maybe yours too!  

Skyy is a CCC alumna and recent graduate of both Harvard University and Berklee College of Music, where she studied African American Studies and Contemporary Vocal Performance, respectively.

Admission is free. Seating is on a first-come, first-served basis.
location: Lang Music Building Room 214-Presser Room, Lang Music Concert Hall, Lang Music Building Upper Lobby, Lang Music Building Lower Lobby

description:
The Physics and Astronomy department hosts a telescope open house on the second Tuesday of each month at the Peter van de Kamp Observatory on the roof of the Science Center. Directions are available at www.swarthmore.edu/peter-van-de-kamp-observatory/directions.

The open house hours are 8:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. during standard time and 9:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. during daylight savings time. The SC-1 exterior door under the breezeway will be open 10 minutes before the start time. The rooftop access door will open at the event start time.

If the weather is bad, the open house will be cancelled by 5:00 p.m., and an announcement will be posted on the Physics and Astronomy department website and the College calendar. Check https://www.swarthmore.edu/physics-astronomy on the day of the open house if the weather is questionable

Open to the public.
location: Science Center Peter Van de Kamp Observatory, Science Center Observatory Computer Room

title: American Communities 1776-2026
description: Swarthmore College Libraries marks "America250" with a special exhibition in McCabe Library. Drawing on art and artifacts from Special Collections, the exhibition explores how family, land, and nation have shaped the idea of “America,” and invites visitors to consider how they see this country and its history today.

title: Staff Summer Reading Pizza Party
description: Come celebrate a summer of reading! We'll have pizza from Swarthmore Pizza, water ice from John's, and prizes for the top-reading individuals and teams.

title: SwatMeets Philly
description:
SwatMeets Philly
Thursday, August 13 
5:30–7:30 p.m.

Independence Beer Garden
100 S. Independence Mall West
Philadelphia

SwatMeets are casual gatherings designed to help you connect with fellow Swatties — those you know and those you’re soon to know. Whether you’ve called the city home for years or just arrived, this is your chance to join a welcoming community of alumni who share that unmistakable Swarthmore energy.

There’s no program, no pressure, and no need to dress up. Come as you are, stay as long as you like, and leave with new connections.

RSVP now! We can't wait to see you there!

Katee Kemether ’23on behalf of the Swarthmore Alumni Council

title: International Student Orientation
description:
International Student Orientation provides international students with added time and exposure to life at Swarthmore College. This program helps students to adjust to Swarthmore, build community, and connect with resources in an engaging, fun-filled program. 

International Student Orientation is required and mandatory for all incoming international F1 visa holders, and international exchange and transfer students. While optional, Americans living abroad and third culture kids (TCKs) are welcome to participate in the International Student Orientation. If you would like additional information, please contact a member of the ISC team!


Future dates for International Student Orientation can be found on the College Calendar.

title: FLI-POP Workshops

description: The Summer Social Justice Institute (SSJI) is a pre-Orientation program that incoming first-year students apply to participate in alongside a cohort of selected Haverford students.

title: Swarthmore at the Dodgers
description:
Dodger Stadium
1000 Vin Scully Ave
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Join fellow alums for a great day at the ballpark. Connect with old friends, make new connections, and bring your Garnet spirit to the stands. Before the first pitch, we’ll host a casual meetup inside the stadium. It’s the perfect chance to grab a snack, catch up, and mingle with your local community. Your alumni host, Grant Torre ’17, will be there to welcome you.

We’ll host a casual meetup inside the stadium before the first pitch. It’s the perfect chance to grab a snack, catch up, and mingle with your local community. Your alumni host, Grant Torre ’17, will be there to welcome you.

Program:

Pre-Game Meetup: 12:15–1 p.m. (specific stadium location details to follow)

Game Time: 1:10 p.m. vs. Pirates

What to Wear: Your favorite Swarthmore gear

Important Ticket Information: Tickets for this event are entirely electronic. They will be securely distributed directly to you through the MLB Ballpark app exactly one week before the game. Please review the official stadium policies on bags and entry before you arrive.

 Secure your tickets and get ready for game day!

All ticket sales are final; no refunds will be issued. Please contact us at alumni@swarthmore.edu with any questions before purchasing.

title: Exhibit: The Hidden World of Fungi
description:
Fungi have long been entangled with the history of Swarthmore. Displayed here are items from the Friends Historical Library, Rare Book Room, Peace Collection, College archives, and Biology Department archives, spanning over 500 years of human entanglement with the fungal kingdom. Welcome to the understory.

View the AccessAble USA guide for McCabe Library.
View the AccessAble USA guide for Swarthmore College.

For directions, parking, and a campus map, please refer to Visit Swarthmore.

This exhibit is a part of Swarthmore Fungi Fest, which is free and open to the public. Some individual events may require registration in advance.

Swarthmore Fungi Fest is supported by the Cooper Foundation, Swarthmore College Libraries, the Biology Department, the Campus and Community Store, the English Department, the Environmental Studies Program, the Film and Media Studies Department, Kitao Gallery, the Office of Sustainability, Scott Arboretum & Gardens, with a special thanks to our local mycology clubs, the Philadelphia Mycology Club and the New Jersey Mycological Association.

title: Garnet Day
description:
Gather with friends and colleagues to celebrate summer this Garnet Day! Enjoy a selection of drinks alongside sweet and savory treats.

 

Garnet Day Summer Soirée 

Thursday, Aug. 7

3–5 p.m.

The Inn at Swarthmore 

 

Please bring a government-issued ID. 

As always, remember to wear your Swarthmore gear to show your spirit!

title: Co-Ed Club Volleyball Practice
description: Co-Ed Club Volleyball holds our weekly practices 3 times each week. We offer volleyball at all levels, so come join! Open to students, faculty, and staff. Whether you have never played volleyball or are an experienced player, come have fun with us and see what it's about!
